framework.name: Dispute resolution via cryptographic audit trail
framework.description: Mastercard's approach to the liability/chargeback gap: Verifiable Intent captures the consumer's specific instructions AND records the agent-merchant interaction, creating a clear audit trail for disputes. Selective Disclosure limits data exposure to only what's needed for fraud/dispute resolution. This is the first concrete chargeback framework proposed for agent transactions.
framework.raw_quote: If a dispute occurs, all parties can rely on a clear audit trail to resolve it quickly and fairly... Transaction details can be verified for fraud mitigation or dispute resolution without exposing sensitive information beyond that process.

source.title: How Verifiable Intent builds trust in agentic AI commerce - Mastercard
